screen.guzhenrenext.forge.title: Flying Sword Forge
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.title: Flying Sword Compendium
1. Place a sword item as the core sword.
2. Feed matching swords to fill progress. The default target is 64 swords.
3. Feed Gu insects to accumulate dao marks. z1-z5 tags contribute 1-5 points.
4. Total dao mark score determines the finished flying sword quality.
5. Claim the completed flying sword when progress is full; it is stored automatically.
Z - Select the nearest flying sword
C - Recall the nearest or selected flying sword
V - Restore one recalled flying sword
H - Toggle the flying sword HUD
Canceling training only returns the core sword. Material swords and Gu insects are not refunded.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.quality.d: Flying sword quality defines base multipliers and growth potential across nine tiers.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.quality.e: %s - Damage x%.1f Speed x%.1f Max Lv%d
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.quality.d: Score <10 = Common, 10+ = Spirit, 20+ = Mystic, 35+ = Earth, 50+ = Heaven, 70+ = King.
Emperor, Saint, and Immortal tiers are not yet obtainable through training.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.combat.mo: Orbit (cycle with Z/X flow) - circles the owner and does not attack on its own.
Hover - waits above the owner in a stationary stance.
Guard - attacks enemies approaching the owner within 16 blocks.
Hunt - searches a wider area for targets. It roams farther before leash rules apply.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.combat.im: Gu-derived dao marks form sword imprints that influence special attacks and trigger chances.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.growth.ex: Flying swords gain experience from damage dealt. Kills and bosses provide bonus experience.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.growth.le: Higher levels need more experience, while early levels receive growth discounts.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.growth.br: Reaching the current cap unlocks a breakthrough into the next quality tier, with extra cost.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.o: Benming gameplay is the deep layer of the flying sword system. First guides stay short, while rules, risks, and recovery paths stay here.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.g: A first guide only tells you the next step, such as finishing the bond, checking resonance, or responding to overload. Detailed explanations stay in this help page.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.b: When HUD or a short guide tells you bonding is available, confirm the flying sword is selected first, then use the bond entry. Missing selection is handled as a separate failure reason.
After a successful bond, the sword enters the Benming path. The first guide should then point you to resonance and risk, not dump every rule at once.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.f: If bonding fails, check whether you already have a Benming sword, whether the target belongs to someone else, whether cooldown is active, and whether resources are sufficient.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.r: After bonding, you gradually face four resonance routes: fierce offense, steady defense, agile spirit, and Devour (Refine), which turns cost and risk into value. The first guide should only tell you to compare styles before committing.
Fierce favors pressure, burst, and forward tempo. Choose it when you want to understand Benming through aggression and decisive windows.
Steady favors endurance, guard discipline, and safer recovery. It is the easiest path for understanding Benming with more room for mistakes.
Agile favors mobility, tempo shifts, and finer control. It asks you to read situations carefully rather than handing you one obvious answer.

Devour treats resource costs, aftershock, and backlash as material to refine. Its real payoff comes from turning a backlash recovery window into the next opening, so it is harder to learn and much less stable in guard and recall.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.o: When overload rises, the first guide should only warn that danger is building and point you here for recovery logic instead of explaining every threshold mid-fight.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.b: Backlash belongs to the overload loop's penalty segment. When HUD shows Backlash, overload is forcibly dragging you out of pressure; it is not the aftershock tail from burst.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.r: Recovery belongs to the overload loop's rebuild segment. When HUD shows Recovery, you are restabilizing resources and tempo after backlash; it is not the same phase as burst aftershock. For Devour, this recovery window can even be refined into the next opening.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.b: A first guide saying burst is ready only means the window exists. You still decide whether the current overload, resources, and battlefield justify spending it.
Burst is where Benming delivers its highlight moment, but it is never free. Plan for the cost that follows instead of reading readiness as unconditional permission.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.a: Aftershock belongs to the burst tail. It marks the drop and cost after a burst, not overload backlash or recovery. If a guide tells you aftershock is active, return here to understand why pressure cannot continue immediately.
screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.benming.f: No sword selected: choose a clear target first, or Benming actions will not enter the formal flow.
Not bonded: resonance, burst, and aftershock stay unavailable until the Benming bond exists.
Bond cooldown active: the system is still resolving a previous ritual, detach, or penalty window.
Insufficient resources: one or more key resources are missing. Refill first, then review the action cost here.
Resonance locked: complete the required upstream step before trying to switch or confirm a path.
Overload too high: this is a warning to recover tempo, not a sign to keep forcing output.
Burst not ready: the window has not formed yet, or the trigger condition is still missing.
Burst cooldown active: the previous highlight phase ended and the system is still in its cooldown tail.
Aftershock active: the post-burst recovery phase has not ended yet.
Sword withdrawn or illegally detached: restore the sword state before trying Benming actions again.
message.guzhenrenext.flyingsword.benming: Finish the Benming bond first, then check Help.
Benming path entered. Check Help for the next step.
Bonding is blocked. Check Benming Help for the reason.
First resonance choice: compare the four paths in Help.
Overload is rising. Check recovery guidance in Help.
Backlash triggered. Open Help for recovery guidance.
Burst window is open. Review Help before committing.
Aftershock is active. Use Help to review the penalty phase.

screen.guzhenrenext.forge.help.resonance: Focuses on frontal pressure and proactive strikes, emphasizing burst windows and output tempo.
Warm red-gold, signaling edge-forward pressure and attack momentum.
HUD cues: Edge Cut / Break Edge / Blade Unset. Edge Cut marks burst-ready, Break Edge marks danger from overcommitting, and Blade Unset marks the aftershock tail before your edge fully settles.
Focuses on pressure resistance and guarded counterplay, emphasizing formation stability and sustain.
Cool cyan-gold, signaling composure, guarding, and structural support.

HUD cues: Domain Set / Flow Break / Flow Return. Domain Set marks the steady burst window, Flow Break marks danger that will snap your stable flow, and Flow Return marks the recovery tail restoring stability.
Focuses on mobility and rhythm shifts, emphasizing nimble transitions and precision control.
Lively cyan-blue, signaling swift flow and adaptive finesse.

HUD cues: Beat Seized / Beat Lost / Beat Reset. Beat Seized marks the burst beat, Beat Lost marks danger from missing the window, and Beat Reset marks the aftershock tail while rhythm is being rebuilt.
Turns resource cost, aftershock, and backlash into value, especially by converting recovery after a penalty into a new payoff window.
Ash-violet with molten gold, signaling costs burned into power and risk forged into gain.

HUD cues: Forged Opening / Furnace Bites / Embers Recast. Forged Opening means the route has burned cost back into a new opening; Furnace Bites means current danger will make the refining furnace turn on you; Embers Recast means burst embers are still being worked back through the furnace, not the overload loop's Backlash / Recovery.
